title: haplopappus laceratus
intro: Haplopappus laceratus, also known as Rocky Mountain goldenweed, is a flowering plant species native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Haplopappus laceratus include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as reducing fever and pain. Haplopappus laceratus is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and has also been used in the form of salves and tinctures for topical application.
